## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, let's wrap this up. We've been talking about scale and leverage. So scale is the idea of your business being able to grow and continue to grow, whether it can double in size or quadruple in size or 10X in size or 100X in size. I think I have 10X and then I have 10X again and then I have 10X again. So I think that's like 1000X from where I was initially in like the first year, first year and a half. And we've also talked about leverage and that's this idea of you can keep on making more, either dollars or income at the same time that you're doing less and that you're working in your unique ability, that you're working in your genius zone, that you're spending less time doing the stuff that you don't like to do. And more time doing the stuff that you love to do that where time just disappears. And so that's what this is all about. And I gave you a bunch of different strategies and things to think about as you're growing your business, as you're looking at projects to take on and as you're thinking about ways to expand, how to expand your marketing, how to expand your product line. And I talked quite a bit about always doing this in light of creating what you want, creating the life you want, creating the lifestyle that you want, building the business you want, serving the people that you wanna serve, working with the people that you wanna work with. And so one other thing I just wanna touch on here is just the idea of strategic planning. And this is, it's really easy to cut up in the day to day and be chasing down today's problems and today's emergencies. The things that seem important but really aren't gonna build your business strategically. And I was just listening to this podcast by Jacko Wilnick. And I think it's just called a Jacko Podcast. And Jacko is a former Navy SEAL officer, he's a commander, I'm not positive on that. Highly experienced, spent a lot of time in some rugged situations, leading a lot of people, a lot of Navy SEALs who are just amazing experts. So here's someone who has been a leader to just world-class leaders. And one of the things he talked about is just the idea of what he says, stepping back from the line. So if you're on the line, like the line of fire, like you're in the middle of a firefight, a battle, where you're literally fighting for your life and fighting for objective and trying not to get killed. He said, if you can just step back from the line, even one step, literally one step back from the line.

## [03:00 - 06:00]

Or maybe it's further, but at least take one big step back and then look at the overall situation. To me, that's what you need to continue to do in your business, to take one step back from the line. And that's what I think about when I think about planning. It's like, how can I step back and sort of take an outside view or I like to think about like float up above myself and look down at myself in my business. Get out of the day to day and do this on a regular basis. You should certainly do this on a yearly basis for sure. And I think a quarterly basis would be really good. And in reality, a monthly basis is ideal. And if you can spend a few minutes doing it on a daily basis, it's even better. But step back from the line, step back from reacting. Stop being so reactive, if I can make a judgment here. Just try to not, there's so many things that seem critical, that seem important, that are not important on the strategic level. So step back from the line and do the stuff that is strategically critical and do this on a regular basis. And plan where you're going in a year. Plan where you're going in three years. Plan what your business will look like in a year. What it will look like in three years. Know that that planning, well, it might not turn out quite that way. It was Eisenhower that said, the plan is nothing but planning is critical. He didn't use those exact words in paraphrasing. So the idea of the actual plan that you come up with, it's no plan survives contact with the enemy. That's another military. We're getting deep into military. That's another quote I love. No plan survives contact with the enemy. So in other words, you might put together a great plan and as soon as you bring it onto the world, all of a sudden things are changing immediately. Because the other side gets a vote. The market gets a vote. But the planning is critical. Even if the finished plan has to morph as soon as you get out there in the real world. So do this. Plan out one year, plan out three years. Regularly revisit that plan. And don't just plan to grow for growth's sake. Don't just plan to build a big business just because it seems like that's what you should build. If it is what you want about, that's great. But first plan what you want in your life. And then create the business that will give you that. Lots of resources for that. I mentioned Lifebook in the last section that was a program I went through where you designed all the different areas of your life. I went through a date with Dustin, which is one of Tony Robbins real high end live programs. It was fantastic. It's like a week long.

## [06:00 - 07:52]

It was crazy. Lots and lots of different ways you can get at this type of planning. I like to do, at the end of every year I sit down with a big flip chart and I define all the different roles in my life and what I want those roles to look like. And then I design what I want my business to look like based around those roles. Because remember, you're creating this, you're putting in the work, you can create whatever you want. You can make the rules whatever you want as long as you're being, it's legal and ethical. Part of being an entrepreneur, there's some good parts and there's some bad parts. And the good parts is you get to make what you wanna make. So make something that gives you the life you want. And with that, I'm gonna leave you because that's the underlying core message. With scale and with leverage, leverage is fantastic because it lets you make more money, income, impact, service, make more without doing more. That's the key to being a business. And scaling is having something they can grow to whatever level you want it to grow. And I want you to have both of those things in your life but neither of them happen by accident. They happen by intention. And to get the intention, you have to plan for it. You have to, well first you have to decide. You have to decide what you want, what the life is that you want. Then decide what the business is that you want. And then sit down there and with what you now know about scale and leverage, think about how to strategically create that. So I'm Jeff Walker, thanks for tuning in. I wish you the best. I wish you much leverage and much scale in your business. Let's go get them.
